system_description.overview: CRITICAL NOTE: The system description section of this report was NOT customized — it contains the Accorp Partners template boilerplate text (highlighted in yellow with editing instructions still visible) describing a generic 'AI-automated compliance platform.' The actual product is described in the auditor scope as: 'Lilee is the AI platform for health plans in the US to help streamline prior authorizations, claims and other payer related workflows.' Infrastructure is on Supabase (carve-out subservice organization).

products.Lilee (per auditor scope): AI platform for US health plans to streamline prior authorizations, claims and other payer-related workflows
products.Template placeholder (NOT actual): AI-automated compliance management platform for SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001, PCI DSS — THIS IS TEMPLATE BOILERPLATE, NOT LILEE'S ACTUAL PRODUCT

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Supabase — carve-out method (subservice organization with its own SOC 2 report)
regions: Not specifically named; multi-AZ described via Supabase
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (spins up multiple servers across all availability zones in Supabase on failover)
infrastructure.cdn: None mentioned (sample network diagram was not replaced with actual diagram)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in Supabase VPC with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). IDS and IPS via Supabase.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Not described (network diagram is a placeholder sample — not actual architecture)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network; reviewed annually by IT management. Firewalls and IDS configured to prevent unauthorized access and detect external security threats (CC6.6.1).
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Supabase to identify and protect against threats
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; email scanning to prevent malicious scripts
network_architecture.topology: PLACEHOLDER — The network diagram in this report is a generic sample template that was not replaced with Lilee's actual network diagram. Topology unknown beyond: Internet -> Supabase VPC (multi-AZ). VPN/bastion host for remote access.

application_architecture.pattern: Not disclosed (system description is template boilerplate; actual architecture not described)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development and production environments; changes tested before migration to production (CC8.1.4)

file_storage: Not specifically named; Supabase storage implied
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not specified (backup policy exists; backup and restoration logs maintained for defined retention periods)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Supabase password policy for server/database accounts; Active Directory implied
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1); MFA required to access source code management tool (CC8.1.3)
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination (CC6.1.4)
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Supabase password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; account lockout after failed attempts; no guest/anonymous logins

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (Supabase) used to encrypt data at rest (CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: Supabase KMS; access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA access; branch rules configured — CC8.1.3)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to production (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback (baseline configurations retained as code — CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO/Management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance (CC7.5.1)

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tools for capacity/anomaly detection; predefined threshold alerts)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Supabase logging (implied); logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configuration changes (CC7.2.1)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; resolved via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary infrastructure subservice — database, auth, storage, VPC, KMS, multi-AZ

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; definitions updated weekly); also on Mac workstations/laptops (CC6.8.1)
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually (CC7.1.2)
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party; high/critical findings remediated (CC4.1.1)

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center; Supabase is responsible for physical security
A1.2 — Environmental protections; managed by building management/Supabase
Processing Integrity — determined not relevant to the system
Privacy — determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC6.1.1: MFA enforced for all administrative access to production platform
CC6.1.4: Access revoked within one business day of termination
CC6.1.5: Cloud provider KMS used to encrypt data at rest
CC6.2.2: Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
CC6.4.1: Physical security delegated to Supabase; SOC 2 report reviewed annually
CC6.6.1: Firewalls and IDS deployed to prevent unauthorized access and detect external threats
CC7.4.1: BCDR policy tested annually with multiple loss scenarios including AZ-level failover
CC8.1.2: Branch protection — no production changes without additional approval
CC8.1.3: Source code management with MFA
CC8.1.4: Separate dev/test environments; changes tested before migration to production
CC9.2.1: Annual SOC report reviews of subservice organizations; quarterly vendor risk assessments for non-SOC vendors

infrastructure.rationale: Entirely delegated to Supabase as subservice org. Network diagram is a placeholder sample — actual architecture unknown beyond Supabase VPC + multi-AZ. No CDN, no additional cloud, no named region. Severely penalized for incomplete report content.

application_architecture.rationale: System description is template boilerplate — actual application architecture completely unknown. No languages, frameworks, or compute services described beyond Supabase. Network diagram not customized. Scored minimally due to missing content.

data_layer.rationale: Supabase for database/storage. Backup policy exists but frequency not specified. Per-tenant segregation confirmed. No caching or data warehouse. Limited disclosure due to incomplete report.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen testing, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS via Supabase, firewalls, MFA for admin access and source code, antivirus on servers and endpoints, quarterly access reviews, 1-day revocation SLA, KMS encryption. No WAF named, no SIEM named, no cybersecurity insurance. Controls are real and substantive despite incomplete system description.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments, emergency change process, config-as-code (unnamed), automatic patch management, monthly reviews. CI/CD tool, IaC tool, source control all unnamed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, reviewed and tested annually with multiple loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover via Supabase (spinning up servers across all AZs). No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region, backup frequency unspecified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only Supabase identified as subservice vendor. No other vendors named (application architecture section was not customized). Extremely minimal vendor disclosure.

overall.rationale: This report has a CRITICAL DEFECT: the system description section was not customized from the Accorp template, the network diagram is a placeholder, and the management assertion is unsigned. The report was submitted in draft/incomplete state. Controls section is substantively real (same standard controls as Company 3), but the document cannot be relied upon as a complete SOC 2 report. Scored significantly lower than Company 3 (same company, complete report) due to these deficiencies.

red_flags: CRITICAL: System description section contains template boilerplate with editing instructions still visible — report was NOT fully customized before submission
CRITICAL: Management assertion is UNSIGNED — '[Your Name Here]' placeholder was not replaced
CRITICAL: Network diagram is a generic Accorp sample placeholder — actual infrastructure architecture not disclosed
This appears to be a DUPLICATE/DRAFT of Company 3 (DOC_ID: 1-uAmey1-oQkzWsdM1yP7HhSKXITcCbbPIzf1gh3KSEw) — same company, same audit date, same auditor, but incomplete
CI/CD, IaC, and source control tools not named
yellow_flags: Single cloud subservice provider (Supabase) — entire infrastructure dependent on one subservice org
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
MFA enforced for all administrative access and source code management
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revocation within one business day of termination
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios and AZ-level failover via Supabase
Branch protection with mandatory approval for all production merges
Antivirus on production servers (weekly definitions) and Mac endpoints
key_observations: This is almost certainly a DRAFT/INCOMPLETE version of the same SOC 2 report as DOC_ID 1-uAmey1-oQkzWsdM1yP7HhSKXITcCbbPIzf1gh3KSEw — same company (Lilee AI Inc.), same auditor (Accorp Partners), same audit date (July 22, 2025), same subservice org (Supabase), same controls (29 total, 0 exceptions)
The document was submitted to the Delve platform in this incomplete state — this is itself a significant red flag about the company's compliance process rigor
The system description describes 'AI-automated compliance management' (generic template) rather than Lilee's actual product (health plan prior authorization AI)
The management assertion was never signed — a fundamental requirement of a valid SOC 2 report
The network diagram placeholder explicitly instructs the client to replace it — they did not do so before submission
Despite the incomplete system description, the controls section appears to be genuine (standard Accorp controls template properly filled in with company-specific content)

The product description in the auditor's scope letter (page 4) confirms the actual product: 'AI platform for health plans in the US to help streamline prior authorizations, claims and other payer related workflows' — healthcare/payer market
Compare to DOC_ID 1-uAmey1 which appears to be the completed, properly signed version of this same report
